title: Rootless podman cannot pull image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running `podman pull IMAGE` I am getting the following message: 
```
* Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:57df1a1f1ad841deaf50c8f662d77e93b4b17af776ed66148116607f9aceffa8": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/gshadow): lchown /etc/gshadow: invalid argument
```
This happens regardless of what image I am pulling.  This does NOT happen as root.  This happens if executing `podman run` as well. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman pull IMAGE` 

**Describe the results you received:**
Error message described above. Image is not pulled or available to use. 

**Describe the results you expected:**
Image to be available to use to run a container. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
[aaron@podman ~]$ uname -r
5.8.10-200.fc32.x86_64
```
```
[aaron@podman ~]$ cat /etc/fedora-release
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
```
```
[aaron@podman ~]$ cat /etc/subuid
aaron:100000:65536
[aaron@podman ~]$ cat /etc/subgid
aaron:100000:65536
```
```
[aaron@podman ~]$ podman pull elixir --log-level=debug

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora is running in a LXD virtual machine

comments.body:
What does

$ podman unshare car /proc/self/uid_map

Show?

You might need to 
podman system migrate

@rhatdan 

```
[aaron@podman ~]$ podman unshare car /proc/self/uid_map
Error: exec: "car": executable file not found in $PATH
```

So I `dnf -y install ucommon-bin` and re-ran the image pull. Didn't work.

`podman system migrate` showed no output and the result is the same. Not working. 

```
[aaron@podman ~]$ podman unshare car /proc/self/uid_map

That was a typo, he meant 'cat'

Sorry about the typo.
This means that you are not entering the user namespace. And therefore only have one UID to use.

Could you make sure you do not have any podman processes running on your system?
And check the permissions on 
newuidmap

```
# ls -l /usr/bin/newuidmap 
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 29824 Jul 30 02:32 /usr/bin/newuidmap
# filecap /usr/bin/newuidmap 
set       file                 capabilities  rootid
effective /usr/bin/newuidmap    setuid
```

```
[aaron@podman ~]$ ps aux | grep podman
aaron        890  0.0  0.0 223264   640 pts/0    S+   15:13   0:00 grep --color=auto podman
[aaron@podman ~]$ ls -lh /usr/bin/newuidmap
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 44K Mar 26 13:14 /usr/bin/newuidmap
[aaron@podman ~]$ filecap /usr/bin/newuidmap
[aaron@podman ~]$
```

So something is wrong with your fedora install.

sudo dnf -y reinstall shadow-utils
filecap /usr/bin/newuidmap
Should show setuid.

Also - can you try a `podman system migrate`?
The do the `podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map`  And see if it has more mappings.

```
[aaron@podman ~]$ filecap /usr/bin/newuidmap
set       file                 capabilities
effective /usr/bin/newuidmap     setuid
[aaron@podman ~]$ podman system migrate
[aaron@podman ~]$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0       1000          1
         1     100000      65536
```
OK, things seem good now - I imagine the pull will work now.

It works. Thanks guys. If you have a minute, for my own edification, could you explain how & why this solution worked and how you managed to know what to check? I'm usually pretty good at troubleshooting these things and put a few days into this before coming here. Google didn't help at all.

"there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/gshadow): lchown /etc/gshadow: invalid argument"

Tells us that the User Namespace is not setup correctly.

Usually look at cat /etc/subuid and podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map.  I have seen situations in the past where newuidmap has lost its file caps, although I do not know why.
